title: prunus × lannesiana f. kokesimidsu
intro: Prunus × lannesiana f. kokesimidsu, also known as the Japanese flowering cherry, is a cultivar of flowering cherry trees that originated from the cross between Prunus lannesiana and another species. The plant is used in traditional Japanese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Prunus × lannesiana f. kokesimidsu include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate stress. Herbal preparations of the plant's bark and flowers are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
